items.title: Treat t-SNE as visualization only
items.avoid: Feeding 2D t-SNE coordinates into downstream ML pipelines as if they were PCA components.
items.prefer: Use plots for EDA; model on original or PCA-reduced features with proper train/test separation.
items.why: KL-based embedding distorts global geometry unsuitable for generalization features.
items.example: Save `Y_tsne` for matplotlib only; train `LogisticRegression` on `X` or `PCA(50).fit_transform(X)`.

items.title: Tune perplexity systematically
items.avoid: Running t-SNE once with default perplexity=30 and treating the plot as definitive.
items.prefer: Try a small grid (e.g., 5, 15, 30, 50), fixed random_state, and compare local structure stability.
items.why: Perplexity is the main knob for neighborhood scale; different values reveal or hide structure.
items.example: Loop `TSNE(perplexity=p, random_state=0)` for p in (5, 30, 50) on the same scaled matrix.

items.title: Preprocess consistently
items.avoid: Running t-SNE on raw mixed-scale tabular features without thought.
items.prefer: Standardize when scales differ; PCA to 50D when p is large or n is moderate.
items.why: Distance geometry and runtime both depend on preprocessing; PCA link is linear—see that topic.

items.title: Document stochastic choices
items.avoid: Publishing a single t-SNE figure without stating perplexity, n_iter, init, and random_state.
items.prefer: Report hyperparameters and, when possible, show robustness across seeds or perplexities.
items.why: Layouts vary with initialization and optimization; reproducibility requires explicit settings.
items.example: Caption: 't-SNE, perplexity=30, init=pca, n_iter=1000, random_state=42, after StandardScaler + PCA(50).'
